_comment: Financial-intelligence feed (redesigned 2026-07-23, replacing the old 4x/day world-news recap entirely). 4 checkpoints/day (02:00/06:00/12:00/21:00 Brussels, src/triggers/ai_manager.py's _CALL_CHECKPOINT_HOURS). Each call fetches a large pool of crypto/finance/AI candidate articles (candidate_pool_size) and has Claude score every one on a 0-100 rubric (market impact, surprise, AI relevance, retail interest, viral potential, long-term importance -- see ai_manager_brain.py's prompt). Score >= individual_post_min_score gets its own full post (market card: hook + why-it-matters + tickers + impact/confidence/horizon + bottom line); score in [digest_min_score, individual_post_min_score) gets bundled into a numbered digest thread, but only if at least digest_min_items qualify combined -- otherwise the digest is skipped entirely, same 'don't post a thin version' philosophy as everything else on this account.
_comment_retries: Confirmed live (2026-07-23): a checkpoint call can come back with an empty/unparseable Claude response (a known model quirk, not unique to this account) -- without a retry, that left a hole of several hours with zero coverage until the next fixed checkpoint. max_attempts_per_checkpoint lets a failed checkpoint retry on every subsequent hourly dispatch (not just the next 3/6/12-hour-away checkpoint) until it succeeds or this many total tries are used, then it waits for the next real checkpoint. max_calls_per_day is deliberately generous (4 checkpoints x up to max_attempts_per_checkpoint each) since ctx.claude_budget's absolute monthly $ cap is the real backstop against runaway spend -- this is a pacing knob, not a cost control.
